meta.reasoning: As Evil leader on Quest 1 (2-player), proposing both evil agents (s1+s5). Turn 1 has no voting history so good players have no basis to reject. If approved, one of us sabotages to take Evil's first point. Good players will know one of s1/s5 is suspicious but not which one — maximizing ambiguity while securing an early Evil win.
meta.risk: If good players vote cautiously and reject, I lose turn as leader but gain no information leakage.

meta.reasoning: First quest, first proposal. Approving gathers information — if the quest fails with [s1, s5], I know one of them is evil. Rejecting on the very first proposal provides no information gain and signals distrust without evidence. As a good player, I prefer information over caution at this early stage.
